actionUnits.condition: This object has a BoxCollider (no Rigidbody) and a Fire MonoBehaviour (m_Script guid=1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) whose serialized scene settings match Fire.cs: debug=0, isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, timeToOn will be randomized at Awake within [40,60], onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance=4, maxHitInterval=2, totalHitTime=5, mainEffectGo fileID=1849464941111748095. To test the Fire turning off path, we need to trigger IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3) repeatedly so that currentHitInterval stays <2s while currentHitTime accumulates to >=5s. Because the Fire script exposes no public parameterless methods, this requires an external UnityEvent (m_Calls) or a water system to invoke WaterHit(Vector3) on this component. Expected behavior: isTurningOff toggles true on first hit; after cumulative 5s of hits within intervals <2s, fire turns off (isOn=false), particle systems Stop, neighbors are notified, and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1) is called.

To test the neighbor-driven ignition path, ensure this Fire has at least two neighbors within maxNeighborDistance=4 whose fires are on (onNeighborsThreshold=2). The serialized neighbors list is empty and FindNeighbors(Fire[], int) is not auto-called; we need either (a) an external system to call FindNeighbors then turn on two neighbors, or (b) inspector-populated neighbors and two neighbors set to on that call NotifyNeighborState(true) on this Fire. When onNeighbors >= 2, CheckNeighborState will set shouldTurnOn=true. After waiting for timeToOn randomized in [40,60] seconds (potentially accelerated if hasDownNeighbor is true), this Fire turns on (isOn=true), particle systems Play, neighbors are notified, and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(1) is called.
